subject: Human Resources
title: The Civil Rights Act of 1964
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Civil Rights Act of 1964.

hook.text: Before 1964, it was perfectly legal in the U.S. to refuse to hire someone because of their race or gender.

buildup.text: The Civil Rights Act banned discrimination in employment based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.

discovery.text: Title VII created the EEOC—the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission—to enforce workplace anti-discrimination.

twist.text: The law didn't end discrimination. But it gave victims legal recourse and forced companies to change policies.

climax.text: HR departments expanded dramatically—companies needed compliance systems to avoid violating the new law.

punchline.text: One law made workplace discrimination a federal crime.

quiz.question: What did Title VII of the Civil Rights Act create?
options: The EEOC to enforce workplace anti-discrimination
